dashboard.label: MCP Detection (REST)
dashboard.description: v1.1 — broader, multi-signal MCP detection. Catches custom / community / non-official MCP clients (no official provenance, generic user-agent) on the REST side via splunkd_access. Splunk's reserved identities (admin, splunk-system-user, ...) are excluded by default via mcp_excluded_users.csv.

panel.title: MCP / agent clients detected by user-agent

search.query: `mcp_rest_clients` | stats count as requests dc(uri_path) as distinct_endpoints values(user) as users by client_label, useragent | sort - requests

html.div:
v1.0 limitation: MCP-Watch only saw the official Splunk MCP Server — it keys on the provenance="MCP:Splunk_MCP_Server:<tool>" marker and a dedicated service account.
Real world: custom / community MCP servers and federation gateways often carry no provenance and show up under a generic user-agent (python-httpx, Go-http-client, ...). v1.0 misses them entirely.
v1.1 fix: detect MCP/agent traffic on the REST side by matching the splunkd_access user-agent against lookups/mcp_user_agents.csv — independent of provenance. Tune that lookup for your environment.
Excluded by default: Splunk's reserved identities (admin, splunk-system-user, sidecar_agent-manager, nobody) are filtered via lookups/mcp_excluded_users.csv. Without the filter, any user with the admin role inherits mcp_tool_execute and the official MCP Server's own housekeeping calls (which run as splunk-system-user with the same Splunk_MCP_Server/* user-agent as real traffic) would surface as phantom agents. If your deployment legitimately uses admin as the MCP service account, remove its row from the lookup.

panel.title: REST activity by inferred tool (endpoint-based fallback)

search.query: `mcp_rest_clients` | `mcp_rest_tool_infer` | chart limit=0 count over client_label by inferred_tool

panel.title: Top REST endpoints from MCP / agent clients

search.query: `mcp_rest_clients` | `mcp_rest_tool_infer` | stats count by uri_path, inferred_tool, client_label | sort - count | head 20

panel.title: Detection signal coverage — official provenance vs user-agent-only

search.query: `mcp_rest_clients` | eval signal=if(client_label=="Official Splunk MCP Server","official provenance / server","detected by user-agent only") | stats dc(useragent) as clients count as requests by signal
